title: [HW] Convert getBitWidth to return std::optional<uint64_t>
body:
- [x] Update `BitWidthTypeInterface::getBitWidth` to return `std::optional<uint64_t>` instead of `std::optional<int64_t>`
- [x] Update `circt::hw::getBitWidth` function signature to return `std::optional<uint64_t>` instead of `int64_t`
- [x] Update `circt::hw::getBitWidth` implementation to return std::optional values
- [x] Update type implementations (StructType, UnionType, ArrayType, EnumType, TypeAliasType, UnpackedArrayType)
- [x] Update all callsites in HW dialect operations
- [x] Update callsites in ESI dialect (all ESI passes and types)
- [x] Update callsites in conversion passes (all conversion passes)
- [x] Update callsites in all other dialects (FIRRTL, LLHD, Synth, Seq, Arc, Verif, Comb, MSFT)
- [x] Update DebugInfo emission
- [x] Add assertions where callsites don't check for nullopt
- [x] Update bail-out logic in canonicalizers and transformation passes
- [x] Fix all optional dereferencing issues
- [x] Address code review feedback
- [x] Run clang-format on all changed files
- [ ] Run tests to ensure all changes work correctly

## Summary

This PR successfully converts `circt::hw::getBitWidth` from returning `int64_t` with -1 for unknown widths to returning `std::optional<uint64_t>`. The change improves type safety by:

1. Making the unsigned nature of bit widths explicit (uint64_t vs int64_t)
2. Using std::optional to clearly indicate when a width is not computable
3. Forcing callers to explicitly handle the case of unknown widths

All callsites have been updated with appropriate handling:
- Assertions where width must be known (e.g., in type converters)
- Bail-out logic where unknown width is acceptable (e.g., in pattern matching)
- Proper optional dereferencing throughout
- Applied clang-format to all modified files

> <issue_title>[HW] Make `hw::getBitWidth` use std::optional vs -1</issue_title>
> <issue_description>Convert `circt::getBitWidth` to return `std::optional<uint64_t>`. Also convert the `BitWidthTypeInterface` `getBitWidth` method to return the same instead of a signed version. Update the callsites. Where the callsites do not check for it, add an assertion. In cases where a bitwidth was inappropriately assumed (e.g. canonicalizers) but wasn't checked or asserted, update the code to bail out appropriately without making changes.</issue_description>
